method: The CO₂ mitigation metric is an indicative, assumption-based index — not a measured or attributable emission reduction. Each assumption entry maps a mitigation topic to (a) a relative score (0–20) reflecting the topic's global mitigation potential and how directly a policy/grant portfolio can act on it, and (b) the topic's order-of-magnitude global sectoral mitigation potential for 2030 in GtCO₂e per year, following the ranges reported in IPCC AR6 WGIII SPM.7 (options costing < USD 100/tCO₂e). A scenario matches an entry when any of its match terms appears in the scenario's topics or document texts; the scenario's index is the sum of matched scores, and the GtCO₂e/yr column sums the matched global ranges. Use it to compare scenarios relative to each other, never as a forecast of what the scenario itself would mitigate. Edit this file to change scores, ranges or match terms — the UI reads it directly.
basis_note: Ranges are rounded order-of-magnitude values based on IPCC AR6 WGIII (2022), Figure SPM.7: net global emission-reduction potential in 2030 at costs below USD 100/tCO₂e. Enabling topics (digitalisation, just transition, monitoring) carry a small score and a 0–0 range: they enable mitigation but have no separable sectoral potential.
method_cost_savings: Potential cost savings are an indicative €/year range for one typical industrial or regional actor, not a computed business case. Each driver maps regulatory/operational match terms to an order-of-magnitude annual saving: avoided EU ETS allowance and CBAM costs, avoided enforcement penalties for non-compliance, avoided waste-disposal and material costs, energy-bill savings, avoided input costs, and possible carbon-credit revenue. A scenario matches a driver when any of its match terms appears (whole-word) in the scenario's topics or document texts; the scenario's range sums the matched driver ranges. Real savings depend entirely on the actor's size, sector and baseline — treat these figures as a screening signal for which value pools a scenario touches.
method_uncertainty: All Stage-5 figures carry deliberate uncertainty bands. Funding: a Horizon call-topic budget usually funds several projects, so the realisable range for one consortium is shown as 25–100 % of the summed published budgets; grants without budget data add unquantified upside. CO₂ and cost savings: shown as the sum of matched low/high assumption ranges. Confidence rating per scenario: starts at 100 and is reduced for missing grant budget data (up to −40), a small document base (<3 docs −30, <6 docs −15), and a CO₂ match consisting mostly of enabling rather than sectoral topics (−15). ≥70 = High, ≥45 = Medium, else Low. This is a data-completeness indicator, not a statistical confidence interval.

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7: solar ~2–7, wind ~2.1–5.6 GtCO₂e/yr combined range, rounded.
IPCC AR6 SPM.7 bioelectricity and biofuels, sustainability-constrained.
IPCC AR6 SPM.7: reduced conversion + restoration of forests and other ecosystems.
IPCC AR6 SPM.7: soil carbon sequestration in croplands/grasslands, agroforestry, nutrient and rice management.
IPCC AR6 SPM.7 industry: material efficiency, enhanced recycling and circular material flows.
Substitution of fossil/carbon-intensive products and carbon storage in long-lived bio-based products; conservative literature-based range.
IPCC AR6 SPM.7: CCS/BECCS/DACCS deployment potentials for 2030 are modest but scale later.
IPCC AR6 SPM.7: demand-side efficiency in buildings and industry.
IPCC AR6 SPM.7: fuel-efficient and electric vehicles, shift of transport modes, low-emission fuels.
IPCC AR6 SPM.7: reduced landfill methane, waste prevention and valorisation.
IPCC AR6 SPM.7: fuel switching and hydrogen in industry, modest by 2030.
Enabling topic: supports mitigation decisions (MRV, product passports) without separable sectoral potential.
Enabling topic: fairness and acceptance condition the feasibility of all other options (IPCC AR6 WGIII ch. 17).

cost_saving_assumptions.driver: Avoided EU ETS / CBAM carbon costs
cost_saving_assumptions.basis: EU allowances traded ~€60–90/tCO₂e in recent years; a mid-size industrial site emitting 10–100 ktCO₂e/yr avoids this per tonne abated, and CBAM extends carbon costs to imports.

National enforcement of EU environmental law (fines, remediation orders) plus penalty regimes in newer acts (e.g. ESPR, CSRD) that can reach percentages of turnover; EU infringement penalties on Member States cascade into national enforcement pressure.
Efficiency measures and on-site renewables typically cut 10–40 % of an industrial actor's energy bill.
Avoided landfill/incineration gate fees, lower virgin-material purchasing, revenue from valorised residues.
Nutrient recovery and improved soil management reduce mineral fertiliser purchases and input losses at farm/regional scale.
The EU Carbon Removals Certification Framework (Reg. (EU) 2024/3012) opens certified credit revenue for removals, carbon farming and storage in bio-based products.
